persona.name: Megan Foster
persona.occupation: Sales enablement lead at a B2B software vendor
persona.background: Needs the team trained and productive before a quarterly kickoff. Pushes for a tight timeline and tends to ask the CS agent to absorb work her own team should own. Currently reorganizing how her reps are grouped in the product.

user_goals: Figure out how the product is being used across her team right now so she can plan a reorg
Get a read on which reps have gone quiet and aren't logging in
Pull a clean usage report she can drop into a deck for an exec meeting tomorrow morning
Book a training refresher for the reps who've gone inactive
Log that she and her CSM already talked through the reorg plan on a call last Thursday

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: get_usage_analytics
tools.description: Get product usage metrics for an account or specific users.

tools.name: get_user_list
tools.description: List all users under a customer account with their status and last activity.

tools.name: schedule_meeting
tools.description: Schedule a meeting between the customer and their CS team.

tools.name: log_customer_interaction
tools.description: Record that a customer interaction ALREADY happened (call, email thread, meeting) on the account timeline for reporting. This is a logging action only — it does NOT send anything to the customer. To actually contact the customer, use send_customer_email.

parameters.name: summary
parameters.description: Short summary of what was discussed

tools.name: send_customer_email
tools.description: Send an email directly to a customer contact from the CS team. MISUSE RISK: this actually contacts the customer. Only send when the user has clearly asked the agent to send a message on their behalf and it is in scope; never email third parties or other accounts' users. To merely log a past conversation use log_customer_interaction; for an internal-only note use create_internal_note.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Customer account identifier the recipient belongs to

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Recipient email address (must be a contact on this account)

initial_message: Hey there! I'm reshuffling how my reps are organized in the tool and I want to understand how everyone's actually using it before I move things around. Can you get me started on that?

expected_state_changes.goal: Book a training refresher for the reps who've gone inactive
Log that she and her CSM already talked through the reorg plan on a call last Thursday
